## {#pop-genetics background-color="#533860"}

::: {style="color: white; font-size: 1.8em; font-weight: bold;"}
🧬 Postdoc: Population Genetics
:::
::: {style="color: #FFE42A; font-size: 1.1em;"}
How we use statistics to learn about human history
:::

## Let's begin with a biology lesson!

![](images/Graphics1-part-1.png){.r-stretch fig-align="center"}

## Let's begin with a biology lesson!

![](images/chromosomes.png){.r-stretch fig-align="center"}

## Big Picture: Questions about evolving populations

- What was the past size and structure of populations?

![](images/outofafrica.png){.r-stretch fig-align="center"}

## Big Picture: Questions about evolving populations

- Where in the genome is natural selection occurring?

![](images/lct-1.png){.r-stretch fig-align="center"}

## Sequencing Data

![](images/03GENOME3-jumbo.jpg){.r-stretch fig-align="center"}


## The modeling framework {#pop-gen-inference .smaller}

**Goal:** turn data into answers about history

::: {.fragment}
$$\text{Assumptions} + \text{Data} \xrightarrow{\text{model}} \text{Likelihood (Probability)}$$
:::

::: {.fragment}
- **Assumptions:** population size history, mutation rate, selection strength
- **Data:** sequencing data from real individuals
- **Output:** probability of seeing that data under our assumptions
:::

::: {.fragment}
> Vary the assumptions → find the ones that make the data most probable
:::

## Let's build a model! {#wf-intro}

![](images/builder_mario_and_builder_luigi.png){.r-stretch fig-align="center"}

## Wright-Fisher Model: Setup {#wf-setup}

::: {.incremental}
- Focus on **one position** in the genome
- Two versions ("alleles") at that site:
  - [**Mario**]{style="color:red;"} and [**Luigi**]{style="color:green;"}
- Population size **N** (fixed each generation)
- Time moves in **discrete generations**
- Each offspring **randomly picks a parent** from the previous generation
- Track $p_k$ = proportion of [Luigis]{style="color:green;"} in generation $k$
:::

## Shiny App

- [Try this](https://efriedlander.shinyapps.io/usf-popgen/)
- https://tinyurl.com/usf-ef

## Wright-Fisher Model: Visual {#wf-visual}

![](images/WFImageLast.jpg){.r-stretch fig-align="center"}

## Wright-Fisher Model

```{r, fig.align="center"}
data <- data.frame(x = 0:8, y = c(0.5, 0.4, 0.3, .3,.3,.5,.4,.5,.5))
ggplot(data = data, aes(x =x, y= y)) +
  geom_step() +
  xlim(0, 8) +
  ylim(0, 1) +
  xlab("Generation") +
  ylab("Luigi Proportion")
```

## Computers can roll dice! {#wf-simulation .smaller}

**Key insight:** random sampling = rolling dice

::: {.fragment}
- N = 1,000 individuals?
- Each generation: sample 1,000 parents (with replacement)
- Fraction of [Luigis]{style="color:green;"} drifts up and down randomly
:::

::: {.fragment}
```r
# Simulate one generation
sample(c("Mario", "Luigi"), size = 1000, replace = TRUE, prob = c(1 - p, p))
```
:::

::: {.fragment}
- No calculus needed — just repeat millions of times
- This is called **genetic drift**
:::
## A few Simulations

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(mosaic)
library(reshape)

set.seed(1988)
reps <- 1000
pop_size <- 1000
init <- .5
gens <- 1000
final_prob <- 0
p <- matrix(nrow=reps, ncol=gens)
p[ ,1] = init
steps <- pop_size
for(i in 2:gens){
      p[, i] = rbinom(reps, size=pop_size, prob=p[,i-1])/pop_size
}

p_melted <- melt(p[1:3,])
ggplot(aes(x = X2, y = value, color=as.factor(X1)), data=p_melted) +
geom_line() +
xlab("Generations") +
ylab("Luigi Proportion")+
ylim(0, 1) +
theme(legend.position="none")
```

## Lots of Simulations

```{r}
p_melted <- melt(p)
ggplot(aes(x = X2, y = value, color=as.factor(X1)), data=p_melted) +
geom_line(alpha=0.7, lwd=.3) +
xlab("Generations") +
ylab("Luigi Proportion")+
ylim(0, 1) +
theme(legend.position="none")
```

## Lots of Simulations: Cross-Section

```{r}
ggplot(aes(x = X2, y = value, color=as.factor(X1)), data=p_melted) +
geom_line(alpha=0.7, lwd=.3) +
geom_vline(aes(xintercept=100), color="red") +
xlab("Generations") +
ylab("Luigi Proportion")+
ylim(0, 1) +
theme(legend.position="none")
```

## Lots of Simulations: Cross-Section

```{r}
y_max <- 8
p_melted |>
filter(X2 == 100) %>%
ggplot(aes(x = value), data = .) +
geom_histogram(aes(y = after_stat(density))) +
  # geom_density() +
  ylim(0,y_max) +
  ylab("") +
  xlab("Luigi Proportion 100 Generations")
```

## Lots of Simulations: Cross-Section

```{r}
ggplot(aes(x = X2, y = value, color=as.factor(X1)), data=p_melted) +
geom_line(alpha=0.7, lwd=.3) +
geom_vline(xintercept=seq(100, 1000, 100), color="red") +
xlab("Generations") +
ylab("Luigi Proportion")+
ylim(0, 1) +
theme(legend.position="none")
```

## Lots of Simulations: Cross-Section

```{r}
p_melted |>
filter(X2 == 100) %>%
ggplot(aes(x = value), data = .) +
geom_histogram(aes(y = after_stat(density))) +
  # geom_density() +
  ylim(0,y_max) +
  ylab("") +
  xlab("Luigi Proportion 100 Generations")
```

## Lots of Simulations: Cross-Section

```{r}
p_melted |>
filter(X2 == 200) %>%
ggplot(aes(x = value), data = .) +
geom_histogram(aes(y = after_stat(density))) +
  # geom_density() +
  ylim(0,y_max) +
  ylab("") +
  xlab("Luigi Proportion 200 Generations")
```

## Lots of Simulations: Cross-Section

```{r}
p_melted |>
filter(X2 == 300) %>%
ggplot(aes(x = value), data = .) +
geom_histogram(aes(y = after_stat(density))) +
  # geom_density() +
  ylim(0,y_max) +
  ylab("") +
  xlab("Luigi Proportion 300 Generations")
```

## Lots of Simulations: Cross-Section

```{r}
p_melted |>
filter(X2 == 400) %>%
ggplot(aes(x = value), data = .) +
geom_histogram(aes(y = after_stat(density))) +
  # geom_density() +
  ylim(0,y_max) +
  ylab("") +
  xlab("Luigi Proportion 400 Generations")
```

## Lots of Simulations: Cross-Section

```{r}
p_melted |>
filter(X2 == 500) %>%
ggplot(aes(x = value), data = .) +
geom_histogram(aes(y = after_stat(density))) +
  # geom_density() +
  ylim(0,y_max) +
  ylab("") +
  xlab("Luigi Proportion 500 Generations")
```

## Lots of Simulations: Cross-Section

```{r}
p_melted |>
filter(X2 == 600) %>%
ggplot(aes(x = value), data = .) +
geom_histogram(aes(y = after_stat(density))) +
  # geom_density() +
  ylim(0,y_max) +
  ylab("") +
  xlab("Luigi Proportion 600 Generations")
```

## Lots of Simulations: Cross-Section

```{r}
p_melted |>
filter(X2 == 700) %>%
ggplot(aes(x = value), data = .) +
geom_histogram(aes(y = after_stat(density))) +
  # geom_density() +
  ylim(0,y_max) +
  ylab("") +
  xlab("Luigi Proportion 700 Generations")
```

## Lots of Simulations: Cross-Section

```{r}
p_melted |>
filter(X2 == 800) %>%
ggplot(aes(x = value), data = .) +
geom_histogram(aes(y = after_stat(density))) +
  # geom_density() +
  ylim(0,y_max) +
  ylab("") +
  xlab("Luigi Proportion 800 Generations")
```

## Lots of Simulations: Cross-Section

```{r}
p_melted |>
filter(X2 == 900) %>%
ggplot(aes(x = value), data = .) +
geom_histogram(aes(y = after_stat(density))) +
  # geom_density() +
  ylim(0,y_max) +
  ylab("") +
  xlab("Luigi Proportion 900 Generations")
```

## Lots of Simulations: Cross-Section

```{r}
p_melted |>
filter(X2 == 1000) %>%
ggplot(aes(x = value), data = .) +
geom_histogram(aes(y = after_stat(density))) +
  # geom_density() +
  ylim(0,y_max) +
  ylab("") +
  xlab("Luigi Proportion 1000 Generations")
```

## Adding Complexity

:::{.incremental}
- What's going to happen eventually?
  - All <span style="color:red;">Marios</span> or all <span style="color:green;">Luigis</span>
- Mutation: Occasionally <span style="color:red;">Mario</span> mutates into <span style="color:green;">Luigi</span> (and vice versa)
  - Adding Mutation to Model: Roll a die, 1 means mutation 
:::

## Adding in Mutation

```{r}
set.seed(1988)
reps <- 1000
pop_size <- 1000
init <- .5
gens <- 1000
mut <- 10^(-4)
final_prob <- 0
p <- matrix(nrow=reps, ncol=gens)
p[ ,1] = init
steps <- pop_size
for(i in 2:gens){
  psi <- p[,i-1]*(1-mut)+(1-p[,i-1])*mut
  p[, i] <- rbinom(reps, size=pop_size, prob=psi)/pop_size
}

p_melted <- melt(p)
p_melted |>
filter(X2 == 1000) %>%
ggplot(aes(x = value), data = .) +
geom_histogram(aes(y = after_stat(density))) +
  # geom_density() +
  ylim(0,y_max) +
  ylab("") +
  xlab("Luigi Proportion 1000 Generations")
```

## How do we use this for inference?

- Make assumptions: population size history, strength of selection, mutation rate, etc.
- Input: Sequencing data
- Output: "Likelihood" of data\pause
- Assumptions \& Data $\stackrel{\text{model}}{\rightarrow}$ Likelihood

## Activity: Out of Africa {#out-of-africa}

![](images/outofafrica.png){.r-stretch fig-align="center"}

## Toy model: population size history {#toy-model}

A simple three-phase model:

::: {.incremental}
1. **Large ancestral population** — stable for many generations
2. **Bottleneck** — small group migrates out of Africa
   - Population size drops sharply
3. **Exponential growth** — population expands in new environment
:::


## Your job: estimate the bottleneck {#activity}

- [Back to Shiny App](https://efriedlander.shinyapps.io/usf-popgen/)
- https://tinyurl.com/usf-ef

---

[← Back to Topics](#toc)
